title: hieracium olivaceum subsp. thlaspidifolium
intro: Hieracium olivaceum subsp. thlaspidifolium, also known as the olive hawkweed, is a subspecies of the Hieracium plant, a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family. Traditionally, the olive hawkweed has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever and alleviating symptoms of colds and respiratory issues, as well as acting as an antidote for certain poisons. In herbal preparations, olive hawkweed is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to take advantage of its medicinal properties, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it as a treatment.
